rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve the root cause by fixing the reference or removing the clause.
For property associations that do not point to anything, ensure the referenced property definitions exist in the AADL model or remove the association.
If a guarantee expression has type '<error>', verify that all identifiers used in the expression are visible and properly declared in the same annex or refer to valid AADL features.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only use identifiers that are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers within the same annex.
When repairing AGREE diagnostics, prioritize fixing root causes like missing property definitions or invalid references over adding new architecture.
For property term errors, ensure all referenced property definitions and constants are correctly declared and accessible in the model.
Validate that binary expressions in guarantees use operands of compatible types (e.g., int or real).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions with known types; avoid using '<error>' types from unresolved references.
Property associations must point to valid property definitions or constants; otherwise, remove or correct the association.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent type resolution failures.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to add new architecture or components when AGREE diagnostics indicate unresolved references or type errors; instead, fix the root cause or remove the problematic clause.
Avoid introducing new property definitions or constants in AGREE annexes without ensuring they are properly declared in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or non-existent property terms
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing property constants or definitions
Property association errors not related to the current component's scope
